message:
refactor(ffs-core): consolidate btrfs fallocate rewrite via push_rewrite_segment + prealloc-aware materialization

The btrfs hole/zero-range rewrite path duplicated three nearly identical
"insert regular extent segment" call sites (left segment, optional middle
zero segment, right segment) with manual offset arithmetic at each call
site. Two correctness gaps fell out of that shape:

1. Materialization was unconditional. Even when the existing extent was
   a pure prealloc record (BtrfsExtentData::Prealloc, no on-disk bytes),
   the code still called btrfs_materialize_extent, allocating a real
   payload buffer and turning the rewrite into a Regular write — losing
   the "still allocated, still all zeros" prealloc semantics that
   fallocate/punch_hole/zero_range explicitly want to preserve.
2. The middle zero-fill branch only fired for Regular extents because
   the prealloc-vs-regular distinction was not visible at the rewrite
   site, so zero_range against a prealloc extent silently dropped the
   prealloc record without re-emitting the zeroed middle.

This commit:

- Introduces Self::btrfs_extent_is_prealloc(&extent) so the rewrite
  code can branch on the original extent type without re-matching the
  enum at every site.
- Skips btrfs_materialize_extent entirely when the source extent is
  prealloc (Vec::new() materialized buffer), preserving the "no
  allocated payload" property.
- Introduces Self::btrfs_push_rewrite_segment(&mut segments, offset,
  &extent, &materialized, lo, hi) which absorbs the offset/length
  bookkeeping for left/middle/right segments and emits the right
  variant (Regular vs Prealloc) based on the source extent's type.
- Adds two new ffs-core unit tests covering the previously-broken
  contracts: btrfs_write_fallocate_punch_hole_preserves_unpunched_prealloc_extents
  and btrfs_write_fallocate_zero_range_preserves_prealloc_extents.

Pure refactor + bugfix; no public API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
